title: when Shinji awakes
wikitext:
[]: [SPOILERS] when Shinji awakes             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Sat May 18, 2013 6:51 pm Do you find Shinji's reaction when he discovers that 14 years passed believable? Is he upset enough? I've only watched the introduction part, the last I watched was Shinji arriving in Nerv HQ. Still, that's enough, I'd say. He doesn't seem too surprise. I mean, 14 years, that changes everything, your world, your relationship with other people, everything.

[]:             Quote Post by Dovah » Sat May 18, 2013 7:48 pm What I really find weird is the amount of years that passed. 14 years after N3I. 14 years after 2I. Is this just coincidence?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat May 18, 2013 8:16 pm Considering that he's pretty much dead inside by the end of the movie, he's certainly not "not upset enough"... It also takes him a while to just take that shit in...

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat May 18, 2013 8:29 pm I think that at first he didn't really believed WILLE : Asuka is exactly the same as he remember her and use a "curse" as an explanation about it, he's told that the brunette next to him is the little sister of his best friend and as for Misato, she's always hidden under her uniform and big glasses. It's a so crazy story that everyone would have big difficulties to believe it. It's only when he arrives at NERV HQ and see the ruins of the EVA cage that he realize that indeed, 14 years have passed : 00:33:48 {Shinji} This is the Geofront, but we can still see the sky? 00:34:00 {Shinji} Is that... Nerv Headquarters? 00:34:04 {Shinji} What happened? 00:34:36 {Shinji} It really has been fourteen years.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat May 18, 2013 8:31 pm Also note that is the exact amount of time he has even been alive before the whole desaster - and it has negated everything he ever accomplished in the first fourteen years;

[]:             Quote Post by Lennik » Sun May 19, 2013 10:45 pm I think we have to keep in mind that immediately after he woke up, he had a lot of crap thrown right at him way too quickly. He doesn't really have the time or presence of mind to focus on every single particular detail. Cumulatively, it's all just too much for him to single out an issue and angst solely about that.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Sun May 19, 2013 11:04 pm Q has enough freakout scenes. There ones that are there are justified, but another would have been too much. Shinji's reaction to the time skip isn't minimized so much as delayed -- when Kaworu reveals the moon, that's when the full force of the change hits him.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon May 20, 2013 8:04 am ...the movie does make a point of showing a gradual decay there. He spends most of the time in bed and/or curled into a ball, but there are implications that he didn't do much actual sleeping in the second half... and then we see the room gradually fall into dissaray - keep in mind that he usually can't stop himself from cleaning up any dirty room he sees. That amount of huge abstract talk needs some time to sink in. Denial/Shock is a natural stage of grief/protection mechanism, that very much crumbled like a house of cards when he realized Rei was dead. Cue Anger("It just isn't fair! Helping damsels in distress is supposed to be the right thing!"SDAT throw), Bargaining ("If I get these damn spears, then maybe shit can be fixed...") and Despair...

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon May 20, 2013 8:15 am Kendrix wrote: ...the movie does make a point of showing a gradual decay there. He spends most of the time in bed and/or curled into a ball, but there are implications that he didn't do much actual sleeping in the second half... and then we see the room gradually fall into dissaray - keep in mind that he usually can't stop himself from cleaning up any dirty room he sees.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon May 20, 2013 3:14 pm You can take away his Evangelion, throw him into an apocalyptic future, change everyone and everything he's ever known, make his father even more distant, put him through at least two more impacts, but you can't break Ikari Shinji's decorum of steel. You can take the Eva away from Shinji, but you can't take the Shinji away from Eva:  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon May 20, 2013 4:31 pm ...which serves to remind the audience what he was like *before* he ended up in this fucked-up situations, read, a bona fide nice guy who never wished anyone any harm... Bunch of gorgeous naked girls in bathing suit, he notices the sickly-looking one... because she looks lonely . So yeah, that's what gives the moment it's power. Once I've found/downloaded a decent subbed version, I might add pictures on the whole room deal. There's admittedly not much TO throw into dissaray (in stark contrast to that elaborate shot of his desk in 2.22), but... pay attention to the bed.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue May 21, 2013 3:12 am The UTW version isn't good enough?

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Tue May 21, 2013 3:36 am ElMariachi wrote: The UTW version isn't good enough?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue May 21, 2013 3:39 am Shinoyami65 wrote: Or Evageeks's own official translation... http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/13456/SPOILERS-Translation-of-Evangelion-30/340/?

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Tue May 21, 2013 3:45 am ElMariachi wrote: I'm talking about the images. Kendrix want a decent version to take screenshots of the evolution of Shinji's room in neo-NERV as his unravelling progresses. That's why I asked him if the UTW version, which have a version in SD, 720p and 1080p, wasn't good enough. I wasn't talking about the translation.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue May 21, 2013 2:43 pm Didn't mean that no decent version was available, just that I didn't get around to download it yet - which has been remedied by now. Lookie-lookie: The room over the course of the movie: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue May 21, 2013 3:51 pm Kendrix wrote: Didn't mean that no decent version was available, just that I didn't get around to download it yet - which has been remedied by now. Lookie-lookie: The room over the course of the movie: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue May 21, 2013 4:07 pm I'd be more inclined to say that the room was simply built before the time skip, when phones still were of use, or maybe it can also be used for internal communications? ...doesn't look like the place was ever really meant to be lived in, they probably just put Shinji in a random room that was still standing. Then again, they do have a goo dispenser in it. But NERV was always crazy prepared for any and all crazy eventuallities, including, implicitly, having to recolonize the earth from the geofront ("This is the fundament for the new beginning" / "This place was designed as a completely autonomous colony".) And Rebuild!Gendo/ SEELE had all the more reasons to be prepared for such cases, if they foresaw this kind of outcome.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue May 21, 2013 4:24 pm On a side note, I wonder in what state Shinji's eventual room/cell will be in Final, if he had the time to get one before all hell break loose of course. Rebuild seems to love drawing parallel between scenes from different movies : the shot of the SDAT in Rei's entry plug in 2.0 and Shinji's in 3.0, all the emblematic places from NERV revisited after the apocalypse(the EVA trailers main hub where the pilots where briefed about how to fight Sahaquiel, the vending machine room where Kaji made the joke with the "love not knowing genders", and of course EVA-01's cage), or all the scene when Shinji is in the Wunder's Control Bridge which a dark reprise of Shinji's arrival at NERV in 1.0... Seeing the hypothetical evolution of the state of Shinji's room, getting progressively neater with his recovering hope and sanity, could be a nice mirroring of Shinji's stay into neo-NERV, especially if the place the room is situated is in a sort of Lilin City : neo-NERV took away hope and sanity from Shinji, and Lilin City is giving it back to him, and the rooms in both places reflect that. ... or Anno could go all grimdark and Shinji gets even more insane and the walls of his room will start to be covered by insane gibberish written with Shinji's blood...

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue May 21, 2013 4:33 pm XD He's unpredictable. But I wouldn't necessarily call showing the familiar places in ruin a callback for callback's sake... it's a pretty intuitive thing to do to showcase the world has ended. ...on the subject of "bowing", note that he also does it *before leaving* whatever room full of crazy Fuyu is sitting in.... In both cases, we see little of his face.
summary: []: [SPOILERS] when Shinji awakes             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Sat May 18, 2013 6:51 pm Do you find Shinji's reaction when he discovers that 14 years passed believable? Is he upset enough? I've only watched the introduction part, the last I watched was Shinji arriving in Nerv HQ. Still, that's enough, I'd say. He doesn't seem too surprise. I mean, 14 years, that changes everything, your world, your relationship
